---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


Eye Tracking Scanpath Visualization
Data files Used: test1_F_S.csv (fixations & saccades)
      test1_gazetracking_clean.csv (raw gaze path)
David_Carson.jpg (background stimulus image)


```{r}
#install.packages(c("ggplot2", "jpeg", "grid", "dplyr", "scales", "ggrepel"))

library(ggplot2)
library(jpeg)
library(grid)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)

# Load data 
fs   <- read.csv("test1_F_S.csv")
gaze <- read.csv("test1_gazetracking_clean.csv")

# Extract fixations (rows that have Fixation X/Y)
fixations <- fs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Fixation.X) & !is.na(Fixation.Y)) %>%
  select(
    fixation_index = Fixation.Index.by.Stimulus,
    x              = Fixation.X,
    y              = Fixation.Y,
    duration       = Fixation.Duration
  ) %>%
  arrange(fixation_index)

# Cleaning gaze data column names
colnames(gaze) <- c("timestamp", "gaze_x", "gaze_y")

# Loading background image
list.files(pattern = "jpg|jpeg|JPG")
bg_img <- readJPEG("David Carson.jpg")
img_w    <- ncol(bg_img)   # 1920
img_h    <- nrow(bg_img)   # 1200

# Fliping Y axis 
# Eye-tracker origin is top-left; ggplot origin is bottom-left
fixations <- fixations %>%
  mutate(y_flip = img_h - y)

gaze_plot <- gaze %>%
  mutate(y_flip = img_h - gaze_y)

# Plot 1: Scanpath (fixations + saccades) 

scanpath_plot <- ggplot() +

  # Background image
  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  # Saccade lines connecting fixations in order
  geom_path(data    = fixations,
            aes(x   = x, y = y_flip),
            colour  = "white",
            linewidth = 0.7,
            alpha   = 0.8,
            lineend = "round") +

  # Fixation circles — size proportional to duration
  geom_point(data   = fixations,
             aes(x  = x, y = y_flip, size = duration),
             shape  = 21,
             fill   = scales::alpha("#00C8FF", 0.45),
             colour = "white",
             stroke = 1.2) +

  # Fixation order labels
  geom_label(data        = fixations,
             aes(x       = x, y = y_flip,
                 label   = fixation_index),
             size        = 3,
             fontface    = "bold",
             colour      = "white",
             fill        = scales::alpha("black", 0.55),
             label.size  = 0,
             label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines")) +

  # Scales & theme
  scale_size_continuous(name   = "Fixation Duration (ms)",
                        range  = c(4, 18)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title    = "Eye Tracking — Scanpath",
       subtitle = "Circles = fixation duration | Lines = saccades | Numbers = fixation order",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0.5, size = 16,
                                   margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
  )

ggsave("scanpath_plot.png",
       plot   = scanpath_plot,
       width  = img_w / 100,
       height = img_h / 100 + 1.2,   
       dpi    = 100,
       bg     = "black")

cat("✔  Saved: scanpath_plot.png\n")

# Plot 2: Raw Gaze Path

gaze_path_plot <- ggplot() +

  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  # White outline layer (draw first so it sits underneath)
  geom_path(data = gaze_plot,
            aes(x = gaze_x, y = y_flip),
            colour    = "white",
            linewidth = 3.5,
            alpha     = 0.6,
            lineend   = "round") +

  # Coloured line on top
  geom_path(data = gaze_plot,
            aes(x = gaze_x, y = y_flip, colour = timestamp),
            linewidth = 2,
            alpha     = 1.0,
            lineend   = "round") +

  scale_colour_viridis_c(name   = "Time (ms)",
                         option = "plasma") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title    = "Eye Tracking — Raw Gaze Path",
       subtitle = "Colour = time progression (early → late)",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0.5, size = 16,
                                   margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
  )

ggsave("gaze_path_plot.png",
       plot   = gaze_path_plot,
       width  = img_w / 100,
       height = img_h / 100 + 1.2,
       dpi    = 100,
       bg     = "black")

cat("✔  Saved: gaze_path_plot.png\n")
# Plot 3: Heatmap of Gaze Density

heatmap_plot <- ggplot() +

  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  stat_density_2d(data = gaze_plot,
                  aes(x = gaze_x, y = y_flip, fill = after_stat(density)),
                  geom  = "raster",
                  contour = FALSE,
                  alpha = 0.55,
                  h     = c(80, 80)) +

  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("transparent", "#0000FF55", "#00FF0099", "#FFFF00CC", "#FF0000"),
    name    = "Gaze Density") +

  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title    = "Eye Tracking — Gaze Density Heatmap",
       subtitle = "Warmer colours = more time spent looking",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0.5, size = 16,
                                   margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
  )

ggsave("heatmap_plot.png",
       plot   = heatmap_plot,
       width  = img_w / 100,
       height = img_h / 100 + 1.2,
       dpi    = 100,
       bg     = "black")

cat("✔  Saved: heatmap_plot.png\n")
cat("\nAll three plots saved! Open them in your Files pane.\n")
```
Animation:

Eye Tracking — "Through Their Eyes" Animation
Replays raw gaze data as a moving dot over the stimulus/poster
```{r}
#install.packages(c("gganimate", "gifski"))


#library(ggplot2)
#library(jpeg)
#library(grid)
#library(dplyr)
#library(gganimate)
#library(gifski)   # used for creating GIF frames

# Loading data

gaze <- read.csv("test1_gazetracking_clean.csv")
colnames(gaze) <- c("timestamp", "gaze_x", "gaze_y")

# Adding a frame index (each row = one frame of animation)
gaze <- gaze %>%
  mutate(frame = row_number())

# Loading the background image 

bg_img <- readJPEG("David Carson.jpg")
img_w  <- ncol(bg_img)   # 1920
img_h  <- nrow(bg_img)   # 1200

#Flip Y (eye tracker: top-left origin → ggplot: bottom-left) 

gaze <- gaze %>%
  mutate(y_flip = img_h - gaze_y)

# Building a "trail"
# For each frame, we keep the last N gaze points so the dot
# leaves a fading tail — feels more like a real eye movement

trail_length <- 20   # how many past points to show as the trail

gaze_with_trail <- lapply(gaze$frame, function(f) {
  start <- max(1, f - trail_length + 1)
  slice <- gaze[start:f, ]
  slice$current_frame <- f
  slice$age <- seq_len(nrow(slice))   # 1 = oldest in trail, N = current
  slice
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

# Opacity and size fade: current point is big & opaque, trail fades out
gaze_with_trail <- gaze_with_trail %>%
  mutate(
    alpha_val = age / trail_length,           # 0 = transparent, 1 = opaque
    size_val  = ifelse(age == trail_length,   # current point is larger
                       6, 2)
  )

# 5. Building the animation

p <- ggplot() +

  # Background stimulus image (static)
  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  # Fading gaze trail
  geom_point(data  = gaze_with_trail,
             aes(x     = gaze_x,
                 y     = y_flip,
                 alpha = alpha_val,
                 size  = size_val),
             colour = "#00C8FF",
             shape  = 16) +

  # Current gaze point is shown as bright white ring on top
  geom_point(data   = gaze_with_trail %>% filter(age == trail_length),
             aes(x  = gaze_x, y = y_flip),
             colour = "white",
             fill   = scales::alpha("#00C8FF", 0.6),
             shape  = 21,
             size   = 10,
             stroke = 2) +

  scale_alpha_identity() +
  scale_size_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)) +

  # gganimate --> step through each frame
  transition_manual(current_frame) +
  labs(title = "Gaze replay  |  t = {current_frame * 8} ms")

#Render and save
# nframes = number of gaze samples (2042)
# fps     = controls playback speed. 
#           Real time ≈ 120 fps (data is ~8ms/sample).
#           Use 60 for half-speed, 30 for slow-motion.

cat("Rendering animation — this takes 1-2 minutes...\n")

anim <- animate(
  p,
  nframes  = nrow(gaze),
  fps      = 60,            # change to 30 for slower speed, 120 for real-time speed
  width    = 960,           # half of 1920 to keep file size reasonable
  height   = 600,           # half of 1200
  renderer = gifski_renderer("gaze_animation.gif")
)

cat("✔  Saved: gaze_animation.gif\n")
cat("   Open it in your browser or Files pane to watch!\n")

# Saving as MP4 --->  (needs ffmpeg installed)
# anim_save("gaze_animation.mp4",
#           animation = animate(p, nframes = nrow(gaze), fps = 60,
#                               width = 960, height = 600,
#                               renderer = ffmpeg_renderer()))
```

```{r}
install.packages(c("ggplot2","jpeg","png","grid","dplyr","scales",
                   "gganimate","gifski","stringr","officer","flextable"))

source("multi_poster_analysis.R")   # creates participant_one/<poster>/ folders
source("build_summary_doc.R")       # creates the comparison Word doc
```

```{r}
# Multi-Poster Gaze Analysis — Participant One
#
# Generates, for EACH poster the participant viewed:
#   - scanpath plot   (fixations + saccades over the poster)
#   - gaze path plot  (raw continuous trace, coloured by time)
#   - heatmap plot    (density of attention)
#   - animated GIF    (frame-by-frame gaze replay)
#
# All outputs are saved into: participant_one/<poster_name>/
#
# Required files in the working directory:
#   - ET_RExtAPI-GazeAnalysis.csv   (gaze + fixation + saccade data)
#   - Native_SlideEvents.csv        (slide timing — which poster shown when)
#   - One image file per poster (matched by name — see POSTER_IMAGE_MAP below)


library(ggplot2)
library(jpeg)
library(png)
library(grid)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(gganimate)
library(gifski)
library(stringr)

# Map each SourceStimuliName (from the slide events file) to its
# image filename in your repo. EDIT THIS to match your actual filenames.
# Only posters listed here will be processed — anything not listed
# (e.g. "survey" slides) is automatically skipped already, but if a
# real poster's image is missing, add it here.

POSTER_IMAGE_MAP <- c(
  "David Carson"                              = "David Carson.jpg",
  "Armin Hofman- 1955"                        = "Armin Hofman- 1955.jpg",
  "Jan Tschichold-1938-Der"                   = "Jan Tschichold-1938-Der.jpg",
  "David Carson - 1990"                       = "David Carson - 1990.jpg",
  "Sean Alatorre"                             = "Sean Alatorre.jpg",
  "Paul Rand, 1947"                           = "Paul Rand, 1947.jpg",
  "bookcover"                                 = "bookcover.jpg",
  "Joost Schmidt-1923-Saatliches"             = "Joost Schmidt-1923-Saatliches.jpg",
  "Jan Tschichold-1927-Die Frau ohne Namen"   = "Jan Tschichold-1927-Die Frau ohne Namen.jpg",
  "Scott Barry- 2010"                         = "Scott Barry- 2010.jpg",
  "nancy poster"                              = "nancy.jpg",
  "Barry Ceck - 1988"                         = "Barry Ceck - 1988.jpg",
  "Ella Rosenblatt- 2021"                     = "Ella Rosenblatt- 2021.jpg",
  "Iris Chung- 2015"                          = "Iris Chung- 2015.jpg",
  "Paula Scher - 1995"                        = "Paula Scher - 1995.jpg"
)

OUTPUT_ROOT <- "participant_one"
TRAIL_LENGTH <- 20     # frames of fading trail in animation
ANIM_FPS     <- 60     # playback speed of animation

# Helper: safe filename

safe_name <- function(x) {
  x <- str_replace_all(x, "[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_")
  x <- str_replace_all(x, "_+", "_")
  str_replace_all(x, "^_|_$", "")
}

# Loading slide events & determine poster time windows

slides_raw <- read.csv("Native_SlideEvents.csv", skip = 17, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
slides_raw$Timestamp <- as.numeric(slides_raw$Timestamp)

# Keep only StartMedia / EndMedia rows for poster images (skip surveys)
media_events <- slides_raw %>%
  filter(SlideEvent %in% c("StartMedia", "EndMedia")) %>%
  filter(SourceStimuliName %in% names(POSTER_IMAGE_MAP))

poster_windows <- media_events %>%
  group_by(SourceStimuliName) %>%
  summarise(
    start_ms = Timestamp[SlideEvent == "StartMedia"][1],
    end_ms   = Timestamp[SlideEvent == "EndMedia"][1],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(duration_ms = end_ms - start_ms) %>%
  arrange(start_ms)

cat("Found", nrow(poster_windows), "poster viewing windows:\n")
print(poster_windows)

# Loading gaze + fixation + saccade data

gaze_raw <- read.csv("ET_RExtAPI-GazeAnalysis.csv", skip = 20, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Rename to short, code-friendly column names
colnames(gaze_raw) <- c(
  "RowNumber","Timestamp","GazeX","GazeY","InterpGazeX","InterpGazeY","InterpDist",
  "GazeVel","GazeAcc","FixIdx","FixIdxStim","FixX","FixY","FixStart","FixEnd",
  "FixDur","FixDisp","SaccIdx","SaccIdxStim","SaccStart","SaccEnd","SaccDur",
  "SaccAmp","SaccPeakVel","SaccPeakAcc","SaccPeakDecel","SaccDir"
)

# Coerce all to numeric (file has occasional stray header-like rows)
gaze_raw <- gaze_raw %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))
gaze_raw <- gaze_raw %>% filter(!is.na(Timestamp))

cat("\nTotal gaze samples loaded:", nrow(gaze_raw), "\n")

# Summary table we'll build up across all posters

summary_rows <- list()

# Main loop — one poster at a time 

for (i in seq_len(nrow(poster_windows))) {

  poster_name  <- poster_windows$SourceStimuliName[i]
  start_ms     <- poster_windows$start_ms[i]
  end_ms       <- poster_windows$end_ms[i]
  img_file     <- POSTER_IMAGE_MAP[[poster_name]]
  folder_name  <- safe_name(poster_name)
  out_dir      <- file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, folder_name)

  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("Processing poster", i, "of", nrow(poster_windows), ":", poster_name, "\n")
  cat("========================================\n")

  if (!file.exists(img_file)) {
    cat("  ⚠ SKIPPED — image file not found:", img_file, "\n")
    next
  }

  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  # ── Slice gaze data to this poster's time window ──
  poster_gaze <- gaze_raw %>%
    filter(Timestamp >= start_ms & Timestamp <= end_ms) %>%
    mutate(rel_time = Timestamp - start_ms)   # time since poster appeared

  if (nrow(poster_gaze) == 0) {
    cat("  ⚠ SKIPPED — no gaze samples found in this time window\n")
    next
  }

  poster_fix <- poster_gaze %>%
    filter(!is.na(FixX) & !is.na(FixY)) %>%
    distinct(FixIdx, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    arrange(FixIdx)

  # Loading background image 
  ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(img_file))
  if (ext %in% c("jpg", "jpeg")) {
    bg_img <- readJPEG(img_file)
  } else if (ext == "png") {
    bg_img <- readPNG(img_file)
  } else {
    cat("  ⚠ SKIPPED — unsupported image format:", ext, "\n")
    next
  }
  img_w <- ncol(bg_img)
  img_h <- nrow(bg_img)

  # ── Flip Y (eye-tracker top-left origin -> ggplot bottom-left origin) ──
  poster_gaze <- poster_gaze %>% mutate(y_flip = img_h - GazeY)
  poster_fix  <- poster_fix  %>% mutate(y_flip = img_h - FixY)

  base_theme <- theme_void(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     size = 15, margin = margin(b = 4)),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 9),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
      legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
      legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
    )

  # ── PLOT 1: Scanpath ──
  scanpath_plot <- ggplot() +
    annotation_raster(bg_img, xmin = 0, xmax = img_w, ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +
    geom_path(data = poster_fix, aes(x = FixX, y = y_flip),
              colour = "white", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, lineend = "round") +
    geom_point(data = poster_fix, aes(x = FixX, y = y_flip, size = FixDur),
               shape = 21, fill = scales::alpha("#00C8FF", 0.45),
               colour = "white", stroke = 1) +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "Fixation Duration (ms)", range = c(3, 14)) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0,0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_fixed() +
    labs(title = paste0("Scanpath — ", poster_name),
         subtitle = "Circle size = fixation duration | Lines = saccades") +
    base_theme

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "scanpath_plot.png"), scanpath_plot,
         width = img_w/100, height = img_h/100 + 1.1, dpi = 100, bg = "black")

  # ── PLOT 2: Raw gaze path ──
  gazepath_plot <- ggplot() +
    annotation_raster(bg_img, xmin = 0, xmax = img_w, ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +
    geom_path(data = poster_gaze, aes(x = GazeX, y = y_flip, colour = rel_time),
              linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, lineend = "round") +
    scale_colour_viridis_c(name = "Time (ms)", option = "plasma") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0,0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_fixed() +
    labs(title = paste0("Gaze Path — ", poster_name),
         subtitle = "Colour = time progression") +
    base_theme

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "gaze_path_plot.png"), gazepath_plot,
         width = img_w/100, height = img_h/100 + 1.1, dpi = 100, bg = "black")

  # ── PLOT 3: Heatmap ──
  heatmap_plot <- ggplot() +
    annotation_raster(bg_img, xmin = 0, xmax = img_w, ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +
    stat_density_2d(data = poster_gaze, aes(x = GazeX, y = y_flip, fill = after_stat(density)),
                    geom = "raster", contour = FALSE, alpha = 0.55, h = c(80, 80)) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colours = c("transparent", "#0000FF55", "#00FF0099",
                                     "#FFFF00CC", "#FF0000"),
                         name = "Gaze Density") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0,0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_fixed() +
    labs(title = paste0("Heatmap — ", poster_name),
         subtitle = "Warmer = more time spent looking") +
    base_theme

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "heatmap_plot.png"), heatmap_plot,
         width = img_w/100, height = img_h/100 + 1.1, dpi = 100, bg = "black")

  cat("  ✔ Saved scanpath, gaze path, and heatmap PNGs\n")

  # ── ANIMATION: Gaze replay GIF ──
  anim_data <- poster_gaze %>% mutate(frame = row_number())

  n_frames_full <- nrow(anim_data)
  # Cap frames for very long sequences to keep render time reasonable
  step <- max(1, round(n_frames_full / 600))
  if (step > 1) {
    cat("  (long sequence —", n_frames_full, "samples, subsampling every",
        step, "frames for the GIF)\n")
    anim_data <- anim_data %>% filter(row_number() %% step == 1)
    anim_data$frame <- seq_len(nrow(anim_data))
  }

  trail_data <- lapply(anim_data$frame, function(f) {
    start <- max(1, f - TRAIL_LENGTH + 1)
    slice <- anim_data[start:f, ]
    slice$age <- seq_len(nrow(slice))
    slice
  }) %>% bind_rows() %>%
    mutate(alpha_val = age / TRAIL_LENGTH,
           size_val  = ifelse(age == TRAIL_LENGTH, 6, 2))

  anim_plot <- ggplot() +
    annotation_raster(bg_img, xmin = 0, xmax = img_w, ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +
    geom_point(data = trail_data, aes(x = GazeX, y = y_flip,
                                       alpha = alpha_val, size = size_val),
               colour = "#00C8FF", shape = 16) +
    geom_point(data = trail_data %>% filter(age == TRAIL_LENGTH),
               aes(x = GazeX, y = y_flip), colour = "white",
               fill = scales::alpha("#00C8FF", 0.6), shape = 21, size = 9, stroke = 1.8) +
    scale_alpha_identity() + scale_size_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0,0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_fixed() + theme_void() +
    theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)) +
    transition_manual(frame)

  tryCatch({
    animate(anim_plot,
            nframes  = nrow(anim_data),
            fps      = ANIM_FPS,
            width    = 700, height = round(700 * img_h / img_w),
            renderer = gifski_renderer(file.path(out_dir, "gaze_animation.gif")))
    cat("  ✔ Saved gaze_animation.gif\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("  ⚠ Animation failed:", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
  })

  # ── Build summary stats for this poster ──
  # Determine where attention concentrated: split the image into thirds
  # (top/middle/bottom and left/center/right) - a quick proxy that helps
  # decide if attention was on "text" zones (often margins/captions) vs
  # central imagery. This is a heuristic, not ground truth object detection.

  poster_fix_summary <- poster_fix %>%
    summarise(
      n_fixations      = n(),
      total_fix_time   = sum(FixDur, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_fix_dur     = mean(FixDur, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_fix_dur      = max(FixDur, na.rm = TRUE),
      longest_fix_x    = FixX[which.max(FixDur)],
      longest_fix_y    = FixY[which.max(FixDur)]
    )

  summary_rows[[poster_name]] <- data.frame(
    Poster              = poster_name,
    Viewing_Duration_ms = end_ms - start_ms,
    N_Fixations         = poster_fix_summary$n_fixations,
    Total_Fixation_ms   = round(poster_fix_summary$total_fix_time, 1),
    Mean_Fixation_ms    = round(poster_fix_summary$mean_fix_dur, 1),
    Longest_Fixation_ms = round(poster_fix_summary$max_fix_dur, 1),
    Longest_Fix_X       = round(poster_fix_summary$longest_fix_x, 0),
    Longest_Fix_Y       = round(poster_fix_summary$longest_fix_y, 0),
    Image_Width         = img_w,
    Image_Height        = img_h
  )
}

# Saving the master summary table 

summary_df <- bind_rows(summary_rows)
write.csv(summary_df, file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, "summary_stats.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("ALL POSTERS PROCESSED\n")
cat("========================================\n")
cat("Outputs saved under:", OUTPUT_ROOT, "/\n")
cat("Summary table saved to:", file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, "summary_stats.csv"), "\n")
print(summary_df)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Build Comparison Summary — Word Document
#
# Run this AFTER multi_poster_analysis.R has finished and
# participant_one/ contains all poster subfolders + summary_stats.csv
#
# Produces: participant_one/Gaze_Comparison_Summary.docx
#   - One page per poster: heatmap image + stats + auto-written
#     interpretation of whether attention was text-, image-, or
#     color-driven
#   - A final comparison table across all posters
# ============================================================

# install.packages(c("officer", "flextable", "dplyr"))

library(officer)
library(flextable)
library(dplyr)

OUTPUT_ROOT <- "participant_one"
summary_df  <- read.csv(file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, "summary_stats.csv"))

# ── Helper: classify where on the poster attention concentrated ──
# This is a simple positional heuristic based on the longest fixation's
# location, normalised to the poster's own dimensions. It does NOT know
# what's "text" vs "image" vs "color" in the artwork itself — that
# requires you to look at the heatmap and confirm/adjust the wording.
# The script writes a starting interpretation; edit the .docx afterwards
# if the auto-classification doesn't match what you see visually.

classify_region <- function(x_norm, y_norm) {
  horiz <- if (x_norm < 0.33) "left" else if (x_norm > 0.66) "right" else "center"
  vert  <- if (y_norm < 0.33) "upper" else if (y_norm > 0.66) "lower" else "middle"
  if (horiz == "center" && vert == "middle") {
    "the center of the composition"
  } else {
    paste(vert, horiz, "region")
  }
}

summary_df <- summary_df %>%
  mutate(
    x_norm = Longest_Fix_X / Image_Width,
    y_norm = Longest_Fix_Y / Image_Height,
    region = mapply(classify_region, x_norm, y_norm)
  )

# ── Build the document ────────────────────────────────────────

doc <- read_docx()

doc <- doc %>%
  body_add_par("Eye-Tracking Gaze Analysis", style = "heading 1") %>%
  body_add_par("Participant One — Comparison Across All Posters", style = "heading 2") %>%
  body_add_par("") %>%
  body_add_par(paste0(
    "This report summarizes where Participant One looked while viewing ",
    nrow(summary_df), " poster designs. For each poster, fixation data ",
    "(where the eye paused) was used to identify the area that received ",
    "the most sustained attention, measured by the single longest fixation ",
    "and total dwell time. A heatmap is included for each poster showing ",
    "the overall distribution of visual attention."
  )) %>%
  body_add_par("") %>%
  body_add_par(paste0(
    "Note: the region label and interpretation below are generated from ",
    "fixation coordinates only. They describe WHERE on the poster ",
    "attention concentrated (e.g. upper-left, center), not WHAT was ",
    "there. Review each heatmap image to confirm whether that region ",
    "contained text, a photograph/illustration, or a strong color block, ",
    "and adjust the interpretation text if needed."
  )) %>%
  body_add_break()

# ── One section per poster ──

for (i in seq_len(nrow(summary_df))) {

  row <- summary_df[i, ]
  folder_name <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", row$Poster)
  folder_name <- gsub("_+", "_", folder_name)
  folder_name <- gsub("^_|_$", "", folder_name)
  heatmap_path <- file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, folder_name, "heatmap_plot.png")
  scanpath_path <- file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, folder_name, "scanpath_plot.png")

  doc <- doc %>%
    body_add_par(row$Poster, style = "heading 2")

  if (file.exists(heatmap_path)) {
    doc <- doc %>%
      body_add_img(src = heatmap_path, width = 5.5, height = 3.5,
                  style = "centered")
  } else {
    doc <- doc %>%
      body_add_par("[Heatmap image not found — run multi_poster_analysis.R first]")
  }

  doc <- doc %>%
    body_add_par("") %>%
    body_add_par(paste0(
      "Viewing time: ", round(row$Viewing_Duration_ms / 1000, 1), " seconds  |  ",
      "Fixations: ", row$N_Fixations, "  |  ",
      "Total time fixating: ", round(row$Total_Fixation_ms / 1000, 1), " seconds  |  ",
      "Average fixation: ", row$Mean_Fixation_ms, " ms"
    )) %>%
    body_add_par(paste0(
      "Longest single fixation: ", row$Longest_Fixation_ms, " ms, located in the ",
      row$region, " of the poster (approx. position ",
      row$Longest_Fix_X, ", ", row$Longest_Fix_Y, " px)."
    )) %>%
    body_add_par(paste0(
      "Interpretation: Participant One's most sustained attention on this ",
      "poster was directed at the ", row$region, ". [EDIT: state here whether ",
      "this area contains body text, a headline/title, a photograph or ",
      "illustration, or a dominant color block, based on the heatmap above ",
      "and the original poster image.]"
    )) %>%
    body_add_break()
}

# ── Final comparison table across all posters ──

doc <- doc %>%
  body_add_par("Cross-Poster Comparison", style = "heading 1") %>%
  body_add_par(paste0(
    "The table below ranks posters by total fixation time, showing which ",
    "designs captured the most sustained visual attention from ",
    "Participant One overall."
  )) %>%
  body_add_par("")

comparison_table <- summary_df %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Fixation_ms)) %>%
  transmute(
    Poster              = Poster,
    `Viewing Time (s)`  = round(Viewing_Duration_ms / 1000, 1),
    `Fixations`         = N_Fixations,
    `Total Fix. Time (s)` = round(Total_Fixation_ms / 1000, 1),
    `Avg Fixation (ms)` = Mean_Fixation_ms,
    `Longest Fix. (ms)` = Longest_Fixation_ms,
    `Attention Region`  = region
  )

ft <- flextable(comparison_table) %>%
  theme_box() %>%
  autofit() %>%
  fontsize(size = 9, part = "all") %>%
  bold(part = "header")

doc <- doc %>%
  body_add_flextable(ft) %>%
  body_add_par("") %>%
  body_add_par(paste0(
    "Summary: Participant One spent the most total fixation time on '",
    comparison_table$Poster[1], "' (", comparison_table$`Total Fix. Time (s)`[1],
    " seconds), with the longest single fixation occurring in the ",
    comparison_table$`Attention Region`[1], ". The poster with the least ",
    "sustained attention was '", comparison_table$Poster[nrow(comparison_table)],
    "' (", comparison_table$`Total Fix. Time (s)`[nrow(comparison_table)],
    " seconds)."
  )) %>%
  body_add_par("") %>%
  body_add_par(paste0(
    "[EDIT: Review the 'Attention Region' column above across all posters. ",
    "If most longest-fixation regions cluster in areas with large text ",
    "blocks, the participant favored TEXT. If they cluster on photos, ",
    "illustrations, or central imagery, the participant favored IMAGES. ",
    "If specific bright/saturated color blocks (e.g. the green and red ",
    "patches in the David Carson poster) repeatedly attract the longest ",
    "fixations across multiple designs, COLOR was the dominant draw. ",
    "Write your final conclusion here once you've looked through all the ",
    "heatmaps.]"
  ))

print(doc, target = file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, "Gaze_Comparison_Summary.docx"))

cat("\n✔ Saved:", file.path(OUTPUT_ROOT, "Gaze_Comparison_Summary.docx"), "\n")
```
